title: Mark Middleton Files - Jeffrey Epstein Folder (Box 2, OA #4504)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Mark Middleton Files - Jeffrey Epstein Folder (Box 2, OA #4504)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records contained in the folder titled "JEFFREY EPSTEIN" within the Mark Middleton files, Box 2 (OA #4504, NARA #3164). This folder is documented in the STAIRS/VS Aquarius database printout (Document Number 101694, dated February 28, 1995), which was released under FOIA case LPWJC_2008-0825-F.

I also request any related finding aids, withdrawal sheets, or indices describing the contents of this folder, as well as any other folders within the 6 Middleton boxes (OA
  #4503, 4504, 4505, 4509, 4510, 4511) that reference Jeffrey Epstein.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: Secret Service NCIC Query for Jeffrey Epstein Aug 12 2014

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Secret Service NCIC Query for Jeffrey Epstein Aug 12 2014
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to Secret Service background checks, NCIC queries, or visitor screening for Jeffrey Edward Epstein (DOB: 01/20/1953) conducted on or about August 12, 2014, including:

1. Any NCIC query records from the US Secret Service Uniform Division and/or White House Division referencing Jeffrey Epstein on August 12, 2014.
2. Any visitor screening requests, advance team reports, or protective intelligence assessments related to Jeffrey Epstein on or about that date.
3. Any records indicating the purpose or origin of the NCIC query (e.g., whether it was initiated in connection with a White House visit, a protective detail encounter, or a third-party screening request).

Background: A DEA OCDETF Fusion Center memorandum dated May 18, 2015 (EFTA00173953) documents that both the Secret Service Uniform Division and the White House Division queried Epstein's NCIC record on August 12, 2014 — the same day. EFTA email records confirm Epstein was in New York City on that date. I am seeking to determine the reason for this dual-division query.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: Epstein WAVES Records from FOIA 22-18632-F and Aug 12 2014 Screening`

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Epstein WAVES Records from FOIA 22-18632-F and Aug 12 2014 Screening`
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The four (4) WAVES (Worker and Visitor Entry System) records identified as responsive in FOIA 22-18632-F ("Records related to Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell"), which were withheld in part. I request release of the withheld portions, including the specific dates, visitor names, hosts, and visit purposes contained in these records.
2. All visitor clearance requests or Secret Service screening submissions for Jeffrey Epstein, Ghislaine Maxwell, or any known alias, submitted by any White House office during the period January 20, 2009 through January 20, 2017.
3. All visitor clearance requests submitted by Kathryn Ruemmler (White House Counsel, June 2011 - May 2014) for Jeffrey Epstein, Ghislaine Maxwell, or any individual using a known alias of either person.

Background: FOIA 22-18632-F identified 156 responsive assets, of which 4 WAVES records were withheld in part and 152 assets were withheld in full. A DEA OCDETF Fusion Center memorandum (May 2015) documents that on August 12, 2014, both the US Secret Service Uniform Division and the White House Division queried Jeffrey Epstein's NCIC criminal record on the same day. I am seeking to determine whether this NCIC query was related to a planned or actual White House visit by Epstein.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: Liquid Funding Ltd in Maiden Lane LLC / Bear Stearns Bailout Records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Liquid Funding Ltd in Maiden Lane LLC / Bear Stearns Bailout Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to Liquid Funding, Ltd., a Bermuda-incorporated structured finance entity (IBC RUCEC 29378), including but not limited to:

1. Any records related to Liquid Funding, Ltd. in connection with the Maiden Lane LLC portfolio created in March 2008 to facilitate JPMorgan Chase's acquisition of Bear Stearns.
2. Any records identifying whether Liquid Funding's assets, liabilities, or commercial paper were among those transferred to, purchased by, or guaranteed through Maiden Lane LLC or any other Federal Reserve emergency lending facility (2007-2009).
3. Correspondence, memoranda, or analyses referencing Liquid Funding, Ltd., Liquid Funding Holdings LLC, or Global Securitization Services LLC in connection with Bear Stearns' collapse or the Maiden Lane transaction.
4. Any records of due diligence, governance review, or officer/director background checks conducted on Liquid Funding, Ltd. or its parent entities in connection with the Maiden Lane facility.

To the extent responsive records are held primarily by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, I request any Board of Governors records reflecting oversight, approval, analysis, or review of such records or of the Maiden Lane facility's asset composition.

Background: Liquid Funding, Ltd. had $6.7 billion in outstanding liabilities (Fitch, 2006) and was 40% owned by Bear Stearns. Its commercial paper was held in retail money market funds. Bear Stearns Bank Plc (Dublin) managed its investments. JPMorgan Chase served as Security Trustee. The entity's rated liabilities were reportedly paid in full on April 18, 2008, approximately one month after the Fed-facilitated Bear Stearns bailout. Jeffrey Epstein served as Director and Chairman of this entity from November 2001 to March 2007. I am seeking to determine whether Federal Reserve emergency facilities backstopped this entity's obligations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: Campaign Finance Task Force Memos Blocked by December 2001 Executive Privilege

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Campaign Finance Task Force Memos Blocked by December 2001 Executive Privilege
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All memoranda from the Chief of the Campaign Finance Task Force to Attorney General Janet Reno recommending appointment of a Special Counsel, all response memoranda to those recommendations, and all deliberative memoranda from other investigations referenced in the same context. These documents were the subject of the December 13, 2001 assertion of executive privilege by President George W. Bush, in which the White House instructed Attorney General Ashcroft not to release them in response to subpoenas from the House Committee on Government Reform (chaired by Rep. Dan Burton).

I am seeking the Department of Justice's originals of these memoranda, independent of any copies held in presidential library collections.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: December 2001 Executive Privilege Assertion - Campaign Finance Task Force Memos

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: December 2001 Executive Privilege Assertion - Campaign Finance Task Force Memos
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to the December 13, 2001 assertion of executive privilege by President Bush regarding DOJ memos subpoenaed by the House Committee on Government Reform (chaired by Rep. Dan Burton), including:

1. All records from the Office of White House Counsel (Alberto Gonzales) regarding the decision to invoke executive privilege
2. All communications between the White House and the Department of Justice regarding the subpoenaed documents
3. Copies of the subpoenaed DOJ memos themselves, if retained in White House files — specifically the memoranda from the Chief of the Campaign Financing Task Force to Attorney General Janet Reno recommending appointment of a Special Counsel, all response memoranda, and all deliberative memoranda from other investigations referenced in the President's December 13, 2001 memorandum to the Attorney General
4. All communications with the House Committee on Government Reform regarding the privilege assertion

For context: The executive privilege assertion — the Bush administration's first — blocked Congressional access to Campaign Finance Task Force memos and deliberative memoranda from other Clinton-era investigations. The President's memorandum to the Attorney General is archived at georgewbush-whitehouse.archives.gov (released December 13, 2001). These records have never been publicly released.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell Electronic Records (ARMS, Exchange, WAVES, 2001-2009)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell Electronic Records (ARMS, Exchange, WAVES, 2001-2009)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All electronic records held by the George W. Bush Presidential Library that mention, reference, or relate to the following individuals:

1. Jeffrey Epstein
2. Ghislaine Maxwell

The scope of this request includes keyword searches across the following electronic record systems:

  - ARMS email (2001-2007)
  - Exchange email (2001-2009)
  - Restored Exchange records (recovered "missing emails")
  - WAVES (Worker and Visitor Entry System) — all visitor entry records where either name appears as
  visitor, host, or in any data field
  - Home and shared drives — all White House office components (NSC, OA, WHO, OPD, CEA, PFIAB, RNC_PST)
  - White House Photo System — any photographs tagged or associated with the above names

Suggested search terms: "Epstein," "Jeffrey Epstein," "Ghislaine Maxwell," "Ghislaine"

For context: Jeffrey Epstein attended a Bush campaign fundraiser in 2000, was invited to dinner with President Bush in Manhattan in July 2001, and donated to the Millennium Challenge Corporation. George W. Bush appears on the DOJ's list of 305 names in the Epstein Files Transparency Act disclosures.

I also request any textual records (WHORM, SMOF, or other collections) responsive to the above names.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy
